description:
`src/commands/ready_cmd.rs:52` (`maybe_auto_fetch`) writes an empty `local_dir/last_fetch` file to throttle remote auto-fetch by `stale_threshold_seconds`. It is a runtime timestamp marker, not config.

SPEC §3 declares the home explicitly:

> `~/.cache/balls/` — derived/regenerable artifacts. Empty in this layout; reserved.

`last_fetch` is the first concrete user of that reserved slot.

## Scope

1. Add `Store::cache_dir(&self) -> PathBuf` resolving to `~/.cache/balls/<nested-clone-path>/`, honoring `XDG_CACHE_HOME`. Mirror `xdg_discover`'s state-root nesting (`<nested-clone-path>` = absolute git-dir parent with leading `/` stripped, per SPEC §4).
2. Rewrite `maybe_auto_fetch` to read/write `store.cache_dir().join("last_fetch")`. Create the cache dir lazily on first write.
3. No migration: an absent marker means "fetch now" — same as first-run behavior. Pre-XDG clones with `.balls/local/last_fetch` simply get one extra fetch on next `bl ready`.

## Out of scope

- Other cache users — `cache_dir` lands with one consumer; future markers opt in individually.
- Deletion of `Store::local_dir()` — parent (bl-a11d).
title: Relocate last_fetch marker from local_dir/ to ~/.cache/balls/<nested>/
